mapping.a_element: discrete-time integration steps of the Langevin/HMC dynamics
mapping.b_element: iterations of the quantum-classical feedback loop (short-time quantum evolution)

mapping.a_element: Hamiltonian dynamics that drive the unadjusted HMC sampler
mapping.b_element: quantum many-body Hamiltonian evolution on the processor

brief_justification: The only overlap is generic (both iterate a discretized dynamical evolution), but the transferable machinery differs decisively: paper A analyzes and bounds the W2 sampling bias of unadjusted integrators via a matrix-polynomial characterization of their propagators, whereas paper B is a feedback-control protocol that projects quantum evolution onto a low-entanglement variational manifold to stabilize regular trajectories and expose a mixed (regular/chaotic) phase space. No governing equation, threshold, or estimation principle plays the same role across the two, so no coherent role-for-role mapping holds.
